CREATE TABLE test_dates (id UInt32, date_string String);
SELECT id, parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates;
CREATE TABLE additional_dates (entry_id UInt32, entry_date String);

SELECT a.id, a.date_string, b.entry_date, parseDateTimeBestEffort(a.date_string) AS parsed_date_a, parseDateTimeBestEffort(b.entry_date) AS parsed_date_b FROM test_dates a JOIN additional_dates b ON a.id = b.entry_id;
SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) FROM test_dates WHERE date_string NOT LIKE 'invalid%';
SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffort(MAX(date_string)) AS max_parsed_date FROM test_dates GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, parseDateTimeBestEffort(CONCAT(date_string, ' +00:00')) AS parsed_date_utc FROM test_dates;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS valid_dates_count FROM test_dates WHERE parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE parseDateTimeBestEffort(date_string) BETWEEN toDateTime('2023-10-25 14:30:00') AND toDateTime('2023-10-25 14:30:30');
CREATE TABLE complex_table AS SELECT a.id, parseDateTimeBestEffort(b.entry_date) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates a, additional_dates b WHERE a.id = b.entry_id;
